# vendor/fontawesome-6 — Font Awesome 6 Pro (source of truth)

Built from the designer-provided **Font Awesome Pro 6.7.2 desktop package**
(`fontawesome-pro-6.7.2-desktop`). License: **Font Awesome Pro (Commercial)** — see
`LICENSE.txt`. **Pro assets are NOT redistributable**: keep this repo private and do not
publish these files anywhere public. Self-hosting them inside the WBG ODC app is covered
by the Pro license.

Styles shipped: the **classic** family in **Solid (900) / Regular (400) / Light (300)** —
3,323 icons each, ≈9,970 per-style glyphs. **Brands are not shipped** (no brand-logo icons
in the designs); Sharp / Duotone / Thin also exist in the Pro package but are not shipped.

## Contents
| Path | What |
|---|---|
| `css/core-template.css` | Structural core (base classes, sizing, animations, the 3 `@font-face`) with an `@@ICON_RULES@@` placeholder. Derived from the FA 6.7.2 web CSS structure. |
| `css/all.css` | **GENERATED** by `build/gen-fa-pro-css.mjs` = core template + one `.fa-<name> { --fa: "\XXXX"; }` rule per icon/alias from `metadata/icons.json` (brands-only icons skipped). Do not edit. |
| `webfonts/fa-solid-900.woff2` | Solid faces — converted from `Font Awesome 6 Pro-Solid-900.otf`. |
| `webfonts/fa-regular-400.woff2` | Regular faces — from `...Pro-Regular-400.otf`. |
| `webfonts/fa-light-300.woff2` | Light faces — from `...Pro-Light-300.otf`. |
| `icon-manifest.json` | Slim metadata for `<loop-icon-reference>` (regenerated by `build/gen-icon-data.mjs`). |
| `metadata/` | **gitignored** — drop the Pro package's `icons.json` (34 MB) + `icon-families.json` (110 MB) here to regenerate. |

The Pro **desktop** package ships no `webfonts/` and no web `all.css` — both are
generated here:

- `npm run convert:fa-otf -- <path-to-pro-desktop-package>` — OTF → woff2 via `wawoff2`
  (one-time per version bump; outputs committed).
- `npm run gen:fa-css` — core template + `metadata/icons.json` → `css/all.css`.

The only `@font-face` family is `'Font Awesome 6 Pro'` (300/400/900). The legacy
`'FontAwesome'` v4 name is deliberately absent — that family belongs to OSUI's native Icon
widget and re-declaring it would clobber the widget.

## How it becomes the ODC paste
`build/build-fontawesome.mjs` (`npm run build:fontawesome`) adapts `css/all.css` for ODC:

1. **Drops any legacy `@font-face` names** (`'FontAwesome'`, `'Font Awesome 5 …'`) —
   a safety guard; the generated Pro CSS already omits them.
2. **Strips any `.ttf` fallback** from every `src` (project self-hosts woff2 only, like Open Sans).
3. **Rewrites** `url("../webfonts/X.woff2")` → `url("/TheLoopTheme/X.woff2")` — the ODC
   Resources path, fingerprinted by ODC at compile time (same trick as `tokens/typography.css`).

Outputs → `dist/fontawesome.css` (+ `.min.css`), `dist/fontawesome-webfonts/` (the 3
woff2 to upload to ODC Resources), and a `preview/vendor/fontawesome/` copy with local paths.

## Upgrading
1. Get the new Pro **desktop** package from the designer / fontawesome.com account.
2. `npm run convert:fa-otf -- <package path>` — refresh the 3 woff2.
3. Copy `metadata/icons.json` + `metadata/icon-families.json` into `metadata/`.
4. Update the version in `css/core-template.css`'s banner (the version stamped into
   `dist/fontawesome.css` is read from `css/all.css`'s banner).
5. `npm run gen:fa-css && npm run gen:icon-data && npm run build:fontawesome`
